TLS Status

Stillwater can serve over HTTPS using either a certificate you provide (SW_TLS_CERT_FILE / SW_TLS_KEY_FILE or [server.tls] in config.toml) or an automatic ACME-managed certificate (Let's Encrypt by default; alternate directories supported via SW_ACME_CA). The optional HTTP redirect listener (SW_HTTP_REDIRECT_PORT, typically 80) appears here when active and 301-redirects every plain-HTTP request to the HTTPS port. HTTP/3 (QUIC) layers on top of TLS to give modern clients lower-latency connections over UDP; clients with UDP blocked fall back to HTTP/1.1 or HTTP/2 over TCP automatically. This card shows which mode is active and which ports are bound; configuration is read-only here. Read more →

Read-only summary of the HTTPS listener. Configure TLS via SW_TLS_CERT_FILE / SW_TLS_KEY_FILE for a bring-your-own certificate, or SW_ACME_DOMAIN for an automatic ACME-managed certificate (Let's Encrypt by default). Enable HTTP/3 (QUIC) with SW_HTTP3_ENABLED=true.

Status: Active (ACME, stillwater.example.com) Experimental

ACME auto-cert is unverified by the project today. Treat it as a preview and prefer bring-your-own certificates (SW_TLS_CERT_FILE / SW_TLS_KEY_FILE) for production deployments.

Listening:
  • HTTPS on :443
